exam.name: Praxis Elementary Education: Multiple Subjects
exam.category_description: Exam-aligned prep for future K-6 educators with complete, syllabus-mapped coverage across reading, math, social studies, and science.
exam.hero_subheadline: Study every tested domain in focused 15-minute topics mapped directly to the ETS Study Companion.
exam.companion_problem: Most Praxis prep mixes signal with noise, so candidates spend time on content that is not tightly aligned to test objectives.
exam.companion_solution: We convert the official ETS syllabus into a full learning map with topic-sized notes and consistent concept coverage for retention.

sections.title: Reading and Language Arts
sections.description: Foundational reading skills, comprehension of literature and informational texts, writing, speaking, and listening.

topics.title: Phonological and Phonemic Awareness
topics.description: Understanding the role of phonological awareness in literacy, including blending, segmenting, substituting, and deleting sounds.
learning_objectives: Explain the importance of phonological awareness as a foundational literacy skill.
Identify and provide examples of phonemes, syllables, onsets, and rimes.
Demonstrate blending, segmenting, substituting, and deleting phonemes and syllables.

topics.title: Phonics and Word Analysis
topics.description: The role of phonics and word analysis in literacy, including letter-sound correspondence and syllabication patterns.
learning_objectives: Distinguish among common letter-sound correspondences and spelling conventions.
Differentiate high-frequency sight words from decodable words.
Identify roots and affixes to decode unfamiliar words.
Differentiate syllabication patterns such as open, closed, and CVe.

topics.title: Reading Fluency
topics.description: Understanding reading fluency, its components, and its impact on reading comprehension.
learning_objectives: Define fluency and its related terms: accuracy, rate, and prosody.
Explain the impact of reading fluency on overall text comprehension.

topics.title: Comprehending Literature
topics.description: Using key ideas and details to comprehend literary texts, including themes, characters, and plot.
learning_objectives: Identify the key details, moral, and theme of a literary text citing specific textual evidence.
Analyze the characters, setting, and plot of a literary text.
Make inferences from a literary text and support them with appropriate evidence.

topics.title: Comprehending Informational Text
topics.description: Analyzing key details, main ideas, and relationships in informational texts.
learning_objectives: Identify the key details and central idea of an informational text.
Analyze the relationships among individuals, events, ideas, and concepts in an informational text.
Summarize information and evaluate key claims in an informational text.

topics.title: Text Features and Structures
topics.description: Understanding how features and structures of texts across genres affect comprehension.
learning_objectives: Identify structural elements of literature across genres, such as casts of characters or rhyme and meter.
Use text features like headings, sidebars, and hyperlinks to locate information.
Identify organizational structures of informational text, such as cause/effect and problem/solution.

topics.title: Point of View in Texts
topics.description: Understanding the concept of point of view and how it impacts the structure and meaning of a text.
learning_objectives: Identify an author's point of view in various genres using textual evidence.
Compare multiple accounts of the same event or topic to identify similarities or differences in point of view.
Identify how point of view impacts the overall structure of a text.

topics.title: Text Complexity and Leveling
topics.description: Knowing the role of text complexity in reading development and how to measure it.
learning_objectives: Explain the three factors that measure text complexity: quantitative, qualitative, and reader/task.
Identify features of text-leveling systems.

topics.title: Characteristics of Writing Types
topics.description: Distinguishing among common types of writing such as opinion, informative, and narrative.
learning_objectives: Distinguish among common types of writing: opinion/argument, informative/explanatory, and narrative.
Identify the purpose, key components, and subgenres of each common type of writing.

topics.title: Effective Writing and Revisions
topics.description: Evaluating the appropriateness, organization, and style of writing, and making appropriate revisions.
learning_objectives: Evaluate the appropriateness of a piece of writing for a specific task, purpose, and audience.
Identify appropriate revisions to strengthen the development, organization, or style of a piece of writing.
Identify the interrelationships among planning, revising, and editing.

topics.title: Developmental Stages of Writing
topics.description: Understanding the developmental stages of writing from scribbles to conventional spelling.
learning_objectives: Identify the grade-appropriate continuum of student writing.
Recognize writing stages such as picture, scribble, pre-phonemic, transitional, and standard spelling.

topics.title: Digital Tools and the Research Process
topics.description: Using digital tools for producing writing and understanding the steps of the research process.
learning_objectives: Identify the characteristics and purposes of digital tools for producing writing and interacting with others.
Distinguish between primary and secondary sources, and reliable and unreliable sources.
Distinguish between paraphrasing and plagiarizing.

topics.title: Conventions of Standard English
topics.description: Applying conventions of standard English grammar, usage, mechanics, and spelling.
learning_objectives: Explain the function of different parts of speech.
Correct errors in usage, mechanics, and spelling.
Identify examples of simple, compound, and compound-complex sentence types.
Identify how varieties of English (dialects, registers) support meaning.

topics.title: Vocabulary and Word Meaning
topics.description: Determining literal and figurative meanings of words and phrases from context and word parts.
learning_objectives: Determine the literal meaning of unknown words using context, syntax, and roots/affixes.
Identify and interpret types of figurative language.
Differentiate among the three tiers of vocabulary (conversational, academic, domain-specific).

topics.title: Speaking and Listening Skills
topics.description: Characteristics of effective collaboration, active listening, and engaging oral presentations.
learning_objectives: Identify techniques to communicate effectively for a variety of purposes with diverse partners.
Identify the characteristics of active listening.
Identify elements of engaging oral presentations, such as volume, articulation, and audience awareness.

sections.title: Mathematics
sections.description: Numbers and operations, algebraic thinking, geometry, measurement, data, statistics, and probability.

topics.title: Base-10 Place Value System
topics.description: Understanding place value, composing and decomposing multi-digit numbers, and using whole-number exponents.
learning_objectives: Write numbers using base-10 numerals, number names, and expanded form.
Recognize that a digit in one place represents ten times what it represents to its right and one-tenth to its left.
Use whole-number exponents to denote powers of 10 and round multi-digit numbers.

topics.title: Rational Number Operations
topics.description: Solving multistep problems and understanding algorithms for addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.
learning_objectives: Solve multistep mathematical and real-world problems using operations on rational numbers.
Use the inverse relationship between addition/subtraction and multiplication/division.
Interpret remainders in division problems.

topics.title: Fractions and Decimals
topics.description: Composing, decomposing, comparing, and representing fractions and decimals.
learning_objectives: Compose and decompose fractions, including the use of unit fractions.
Represent rational numbers on a number line, with concrete models, and with area models.
Convert between fractions, decimals, and percents.

topics.title: Proportional Relationships and Percents
topics.description: Understanding and applying ratios, unit rates, and percents to solve problems.
learning_objectives: Apply concepts of ratios and unit rates to describe relationships between two quantities.
Understand percent as a rate per 100.
Use proportional relationships to solve ratio, percent, and unit-rate problems.

topics.title: Basic Number Theory
topics.description: Understanding prime and composite numbers, factors, and multiples.
learning_objectives: Identify and use prime and composite numbers.
Find factors and multiples of whole numbers.

topics.title: Algebraic Expressions and Equations
topics.description: Evaluating, writing, and manipulating simple algebraic expressions and equations.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between algebraic expressions and equations.
Add, subtract, and use the distributive property to generate equivalent linear algebraic expressions.
Translate between verbal statements and algebraic expressions or equations.

topics.title: Linear Equations and Inequalities
topics.description: Solving one-variable linear equations and inequalities and interpreting their solutions.
learning_objectives: Solve multistep one-variable linear equations and inequalities.
Interpret solutions of multistep one-variable linear equations and inequalities, including graphing on a number line.
Use linear relationships represented by equations, tables, and graphs to solve problems.

topics.title: Patterns and Functions
topics.description: Recognizing, extending, and representing numerical and shape patterns.
learning_objectives: Identify, extend, describe, or generate number and shape patterns.
Make conjectures, predictions, or generalizations based on patterns.
Identify relationships between corresponding terms of two numerical patterns (e.g., function tables).

topics.title: Geometry and Classification
topics.description: Classifying one-, two-, and three-dimensional geometric figures.
learning_objectives: Use definitions to identify lines, rays, segments, parallel lines, and perpendicular lines.
Classify angles based on their measure.
Use attributes to classify or draw polygons and solid figures.

topics.title: Perimeter, Area, and Volume
topics.description: Solving problems involving the perimeter, area, surface area, and volume of geometric shapes.
learning_objectives: Find the area and perimeter of polygons, including those with fractional side lengths.
Represent 3D figures with nets to determine surface area.
Find the volume of right rectangular prisms.

topics.title: The Coordinate Plane
topics.description: Understanding components of the coordinate plane and graphing ordered pairs.
learning_objectives: Identify the x-axis, y-axis, origin, and four quadrants.
Solve problems by plotting points and drawing polygons in the coordinate plane.

topics.title: Measurement and Conversions
topics.description: Solving problems involving measurement, time, money, and unit conversions.
learning_objectives: Solve problems involving elapsed time, money, length, volume, and mass.
Know relative sizes of US customary and metric units.
Convert units within both the US customary and metric systems.

topics.title: Data and Statistics
topics.description: Basic statistical concepts, measures of center, range, and interpreting data displays.
learning_objectives: Identify statistical questions and solve problems involving mean, median, mode, and range.
Determine how changes in data affect measures of center or range.
Interpret displays of data like boxplots, histograms, scatterplots, and line plots.

topics.title: Probability of Events
topics.description: Interpreting probabilities relative to the likelihood of events occurring.
learning_objectives: Interpret probabilities relative to likelihood of occurrence.
Determine the theoretical probability of a specific outcome.

sections.title: Social Studies
sections.description: United States History, Government, Citizenship, Geography, Anthropology, Sociology, World History, and Economics.

topics.title: European Exploration and Colonization
topics.description: Understanding European exploration, colonization in North America, and early expansion.
learning_objectives: Identify the key European nations involved in early exploration of the Americas.
Describe the impact of colonization and expansion on Native American populations and colonial development.

topics.title: American Revolution and Founding
topics.description: The events of the American Revolution and the founding of the United States nation.
learning_objectives: Explain the causes and major events of the American Revolution.
Describe the creation of foundational US documents, including the weaknesses of the Articles of Confederation.

topics.title: Major Events in US History
topics.description: Major developments in US History from the founding to the present, including westward expansion and industrialization.
learning_objectives: Identify the causes and effects of westward expansion and 'Manifest Destiny'.
Understand the impact of the Civil War, industrialization, and the Great Depression.

topics.title: Nature and Forms of Government
topics.description: The nature, purpose, and forms of government, focusing on the US federal, state, and local systems.
learning_objectives: Compare democratic governments with other forms of government.
Explain the separation of powers and the system of checks and balances in the US government.
Understand the relationship between federal and state governments (federalism).

topics.title: Key Documents and Citizenship
topics.description: Key historical documents, speeches, and the rights and responsibilities of citizenship in a democracy.
learning_objectives: Analyze the significance of the US Constitution, Declaration of Independence, and Gettysburg Address.
Identify the rights and responsibilities of citizenship in a democracy.

topics.title: Geography and Human Systems
topics.description: World and regional geography, and the interaction of physical and human geographic systems.
learning_objectives: Identify spatial terms, places, and major global regions.
Understand how humans change the environment and how the environment changes human societies.
Differentiate among thematic, political, and physical maps.

topics.title: Major Classical Civilizations
topics.description: Contributions and characteristics of classical civilizations like Egypt, Greece, and Rome.
learning_objectives: List major contributions of ancient Greece (e.g., democracy, architecture) and Rome (e.g., republic, infrastructure).
Describe aspects of Egyptian political, social, and cultural systems.

topics.title: Basic Economic Concepts
topics.description: Key terms and concepts in economics, including supply, demand, scarcity, and opportunity cost.
learning_objectives: Define scarcity, opportunity cost, supply, and demand.
Understand how economics affects population, resources, and technology.
Explain the government's role in the economy.

sections.title: Science
sections.description: Earth science, life science, and physical science, including science as inquiry.

topics.title: Structure and Processes of Earth
topics.description: The structure of the solid Earth, hydrosphere, atmosphere, and major Earth processes.
learning_objectives: Describe the layers of the Earth and its atmosphere.
Identify geological processes like volcanic activity, weathering, and plate tectonics.

topics.title: Earth and the Universe
topics.description: Understanding stars, galaxies, the solar system, and Earth-Sun-Moon relationships.
learning_objectives: Explain the causes of seasons, tides, and eclipses based on positional relationships.
Differentiate between inner and outer planets in the solar system.

topics.title: Structure and Function of Living Systems
topics.description: Living characteristics, cells, tissues, organs, and major life processes.
learning_objectives: Describe the basic structure and function of cells.
Explain the functions of major human body systems (e.g., circulatory, digestive).

topics.title: Reproduction and Heredity
topics.description: Growth, development, patterns of inheritance, and the molecular basis of heredity.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between dominant and recessive traits.
Understand basic concepts of genetic inheritance, including how traits are passed from parents to offspring.

topics.title: Ecology and Adaptation
topics.description: Change over time, natural selection, adaptation, and interdependence of organisms.
learning_objectives: Explain the concepts of adaptation and natural selection ('survival of the fittest').
Describe the flow of energy in food chains and food webs.
Understand how ecosystems are affected by the introduction or removal of species.

topics.title: Properties and Structure of Matter
topics.description: Physical and chemical properties, changes of state, atoms, and elements.
learning_objectives: Distinguish between physical and chemical properties and changes of matter.
Identify the basic structure of an atom (protons, neutrons, electrons).
Understand states of matter and mixtures/solutions.

topics.title: Forces, Motion, and Energy
topics.description: Laws of motion, equilibrium, forms of energy, and conservation of energy.
learning_objectives: Explain what causes an object to accelerate, slow down, or remain at rest.
Describe how energy is transformed from potential to kinetic energy.
Differentiate between mass and weight.

topics.title: Interactions of Energy and Matter
topics.description: Electricity, magnetism, sound, and light waves.
learning_objectives: Understand how visible light waves differ from sound and water waves.
Explain properties of light such as reflection and refraction.
Identify the basic components of a simple electric circuit.

topics.title: Science as Inquiry
topics.description: Understanding science as a human endeavor, the scientific method, and unifying processes.
learning_objectives: Explain the steps involved in scientific inquiry, such as questioning, gathering data, and drawing conclusions.
Understand how to formulate and test a scientific hypothesis.
Use appropriate laboratory tools for measurement and experimentation.
